sources.writer_profile: Francesco Cipriano is a food and travel writer based in Palermo, contributing regularly to Culinary Backstreets. His writing covers the traditional dining culture of Sicily, focusing on multi-generational family restaurants, street food institutions, and the social fabric of Palermitan neighbourhoods.

His writing covers the traditional dining culture of Sicily, focusing on multi-generational family restaurants, street food institutions, and the social fabric of Palermitan neighbourhoods.

sources.article_title: Dove mangiare a Palermo: i 5 ristoranti migliori per qualità e prezzo
sources.writer_profile: Nico Cristiani is a food journalist and critic writing for Scatti di Gusto, a leading Italian food publication covering restaurant recommendations and culinary culture nationwide. His Palermo coverage focuses on value-for-money dining, traditional osterie, and contemporary dining across the city's diverse neighbourhoods.

sources.article_title: La magia di Palermo: dieci luoghi del gusto dove mangiare e bere bene
sources.writer_profile: Marco Colognese is a wine and food journalist writing for Vendemmie, an Italian publication dedicated to wine culture and gastronomic travel. His coverage of Palermo highlights wine-forward restaurants, contemporary Sicilian cuisine, and the city's evolving dining scene.

sources.article_title: Ristoranti a Palermo: le trattorie migliori per colazione
sources.writer_profile: Salvo Ognibene is a journalist at PalermoToday, the leading local news platform for Palermo. His restaurant coverage includes endorsements from local chefs and hospitality professionals, providing insider perspectives on the city's dining scene.

restaurants.name: Casa del Brodo dal Dottore
name_variants: La Casa del Brodo dal Dottore
dishes_mentioned: fava bean purée with wild fennel
restaurants.signature_dish: veal bone broth with celery, carrots and onions
restaurants.quote: The oldest eatery in Palermo and one of the longest-lived in southern Italy, La Casa del Brodo dal Dottore has operated since 1890, specialising in meat broth and boiled meat — the therapeutic qualities of a hot broth are undeniable, says owner Gaetano Romeres.

restaurants.name: FUD Bottega Sicula
restaurants.quote: Andrea Graziano offers Sicilian interpretations of American fast food at FUD Bottega Sicula, prioritising local sourcing — 90% of ingredients come from small Sicilian producers, making every burger a product of the island.

restaurants.name: Osteria Mangia e Bevi
restaurants.signature_dish: pasta fritta (fried leftover pasta with olive oil)
restaurants.quote: I am for tradition. No gourmet dishes, only typical home cooking: each dish on the menu is a piece of Sicilian history, says co-founder Nico Virga, who with cousin Angelo Fascetta honours the recipes of grandmother Antonietta — garlic-free throughout, as she never used it.

restaurants.name: Ciccio Passami l'Olio
restaurants.quote: To see if someone can cook, you don't need elaborate dishes, but a simple plate of spaghetti with garlic and oil — so simple that it's almost complicated to make it, explains Francesco Leone, who left textiles to open this neighbourhood pizzeria in Piazza Magione in the Kalsa district.

restaurants.name: Trattoria Al Ferro di Cavallo
restaurants.signature_dish: pasta with sardines with wild fennel, raisins, pine nuts and breadcrumbs
restaurants.quote: Operating since 1944 and now in its third generation, Trattoria Al Ferro di Cavallo preserves postwar Palermitan traditions — tall flames and hot oil, stacked plates and searing pans, shouting and laughter, serving dishes once affordable to poor Palermitans like beans with celery.

restaurants.name: Trattoria Il Delfino
restaurants.quote: Operating for over fifty years in Sferracavallo, owner Nino Billeci sources fish from a dedicated fisherman in Porticello and visits the market daily: I argue with the suppliers because I want to be sure the fish is always top quality — butter and cream never appear, pasta is handmade daily.
restaurants.article_title: Trattoria Il Delfino: Sicilian Seafood Haven

restaurants.name: Osteria Nonna Dora
restaurants.quote: È solo una piccola osteria in zona Porto, ma gli osti sanno cosa significa accogliere con calore. Senza contare che non troverete un posto migliore per qualità e prezzo in tutta Palermo — you won't find a better place for quality and price in all of Palermo, guaranteed.
restaurants.article_title: Dove mangiare a Palermo: i 5 ristoranti migliori per qualità e prezzo

restaurants.name: Doba
restaurants.quote: Sembra di entrare in un speakeasy più che in un ristorante — it feels like entering a speakeasy rather than a restaurant — with a rooftop offering an extraordinary 360-degree panorama revealing bell towers and domes across Palermo. Chef Domenico Basile supervises a contemporary menu with strong local ingredient focus.
restaurants.article_title: Dove mangiare a Palermo: i 5 ristoranti migliori per qualità e prezzo

restaurants.name: Maqueda Bistrot
restaurants.quote: La prima cosa sorprendente è la parete di vini siciliani che vi accoglie — the first surprising thing is the wall of Sicilian wines greeting you, with bottles at a €7 markup; solid and unpretentious typical Sicilian dishes at around €20 average.
restaurants.article_title: Dove mangiare a Palermo: i 5 ristoranti migliori per qualità e prezzo

restaurants.name: L'Acerba Osteria Dinamica
restaurants.quote: A paradise for seafood lovers in a courtyard near the Mercato del Capo filled with vintage divans, tables and chairs — tuna steak with citrus sauce at €18 and square spaghetti with bottarga at €20, at around €40 for three courses.
restaurants.article_title: Dove mangiare a Palermo: i 5 ristoranti migliori per qualità e prezzo

restaurants.name: Archestrato di Gela
restaurants.quote: Non è un ristorante, ma una pizzeria in rapida ascesa tra le migliori di Palermo — not a restaurant, but a rapidly rising pizzeria among Palermo's best, on Via Notarbartolo near Via della Libertà, with contemporary ambiance and an extensive craft beer selection.
restaurants.article_title: Dove mangiare a Palermo: i 5 ristoranti migliori per qualità e prezzo

restaurants.name: Osteria Ballarò
restaurants.signature_dish: timballo di anelletti (oven-baked pasta with aubergine)
restaurants.quote: Built in the former stables of Palazzo Cattolica near the historic Jewish quarter, Osteria Ballarò serves as an authentic Sicilian putìa — chef Calogero Branca presents street food alongside traditional dishes, with wine curator Gianluca Enzo Buono managing 600 labels of exclusively territorial products.
restaurants.article_title: La magia di Palermo: dieci luoghi del gusto dove mangiare e bere bene

restaurants.name: CiCala
dishes_mentioned: spaghettoni al nero di seppie locali
restaurants.signature_dish: spaghettoni al nero di seppie locali
restaurants.quote: An intimate wine bar and restaurant on Via Sant'Alessandro where owner Filippo Cosentino, a well-travelled bartender, earned Slow Food recognition for his wine selection — featuring spaghettoni al nero di seppie locali alongside carefully curated Sicilian and natural wines.
restaurants.article_title: La magia di Palermo: dieci luoghi del gusto dove mangiare e bere bene

restaurants.name: Libertà Vini e Cucina
restaurants.quote: A casual space in the Libertà neighbourhood showcasing natural wines as 'storie e percorsi di vita' — stories and life paths — where chef Alessandro Fanara prepares regional Sicilian cuisine with contemporary presentation in a room with 1950s décor and outdoor seating.
restaurants.article_title: La magia di Palermo: dieci luoghi del gusto dove mangiare e bere bene

restaurants.name: Trattoria Corona
dishes_mentioned: pasta Margherita con il pomodoro e l'anciova
restaurants.signature_dish: pasta Margherita con il pomodoro e l'anciova
restaurants.quote: La nostra è una solida cucina di mare che proviene da un'antica memoria familiare — ours is a solid seafood kitchen rooted in ancient family memory — with the objective of guaranteeing customers the best fish and the pleasure of the table, says owner Gianni Corona, who runs the trattoria with wife Angela and sons Orazio and Alessandro.
restaurants.article_title: La magia di Palermo: dieci luoghi del gusto dove mangiare e bere bene

restaurants.name: Le Angeliche
restaurants.quote: A small bistro managed by four women in a historic alley of the Capo market, where chef Veronica Schiera offers ricette siciliane e mediterranee di grande gusto — great-tasting Sicilian and Mediterranean recipes — with a small, exclusively regional wine list and charming outdoor seating.
restaurants.article_title: La magia di Palermo: dieci luoghi del gusto dove mangiare e bere bene

restaurants.name: Quattroventi
restaurants.quote: Between the port and the city centre, Quattroventi offers aperitivos and cucina confortevole e realizzata con materie prime di valore — comforting cuisine made with quality ingredients — where sommelier Gabriele Amato maintains 150–240 rotating wine selections paired with chef Filippo Ventimiglia's dishes.
restaurants.article_title: La magia di Palermo: dieci luoghi del gusto dove mangiare e bere bene

restaurants.name: Osteria dei Vespri
restaurants.quote: Located in Palazzo Gangi — where Visconti filmed The Leopard — Osteria dei Vespri has evolved from an enoteca into a gourmet restaurant during Palermo's cultural renaissance, with big, confident flavours and fresh pasta; brothers Andrea and Alberto Rizzo manage 650 wine references at Piazza Croce dei Vespri.
restaurants.article_title: La magia di Palermo: dieci luoghi del gusto dove mangiare e bere bene
